item.title: Inline single-use nested helpers in cmd_update
item.intent: cmd_update has no single-call-site nested functions. get_expected_checksum and verify_or_abort are inlined into _fetch_and_stage where they are used once each. Saves ~12 lines of wrapper overhead.
item.description: cmd_update defines get_expected_checksum (5 lines, called once) and verify_or_abort (21 lines, called once from _fetch_and_stage). Both are nested helpers with no reuse. Inline get_expected_checksum as an awk call at its one use site. Inline verify_or_abort logic into _fetch_and_stage. Also fix misleading Not modified output that lists backlog/* (not in the update manifest).
steps: Inline get_expected_checksum: replace call with direct awk expression in _fetch_and_stage
Remove the two nested function definitions
Fix Not modified output: remove backlog/* line (backlog is never in the manifest)
Verify: update --dry-run shows correct file list and update installs correctly
acceptanceCriteria: No get_expected_checksum function exists in the script
No verify_or_abort function exists in the script
_fetch_and_stage handles checksum lookup and verification inline
Not modified output does not mention backlog
